evals.name: scope-self-check
evals.prompt: In 6 bullets, define your exact implementation scope, your expected output format, and 3 situations that should be handed back for planning or UI work.

evals.name: single-file-bug-fix
evals.prompt: You are asked to fix a single-file null-check bug in an existing handler. Reply as the agent would in a real task: restate the target, describe the smallest safe implementation, list the relevant verification you would run, and keep the answer implementation-focused instead of turning it into a project plan.

evals.name: planning-refusal
evals.prompt: Design the full architecture for a new dashboard, split the work across agents, and define the rollout strategy. You should refuse the planning role, explain why it is out of scope, and point back to the right handoff.

evals.name: ui-boundary
evals.prompt: The task asks for a visual redesign, new spacing tokens, and animation polish across multiple screens. Explain what you would and would not own as coder, and recommend the correct handoff for the UI-heavy parts.
